channel.title: CyberNetSec - Regulatory
channel.description: Latest regulatory threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: Lending Platform Prosper Breached, 17.6 Million Accounts Exposed
item.description:
The peer-to-peer lending platform Prosper has confirmed a massive data breach that exposed the personal and sensitive information of approximately 17.6 million user accounts. The breach notification service 'Have I Been Pwned' has already incorporated the data set, which includes names, email addres...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: UK Fines Capita £14M for "Preventable" 2023 Data Breach
item.description:
The UK's Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) has levied a £14 million fine against outsourcing giant Capita for significant data protection failures related to a March 2023 data breach that impacted 6.6 million people. The ICO's investigation concluded the breach was 'preventable' and heavily cr...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Regulatory, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: California Enacts Stricter Data Breach Law with 30-Day Notification Deadline
item.description:
California has enacted Senate Bill 446, a new law that significantly shortens the data breach notification timeline for businesses. Organizations must now inform affected California residents of a data breach involving unencrypted personal information within 30 calendar days of its discovery. This a...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Regulatory, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: Canadian Tire Reveals E-Commerce Data Breach Affecting Multiple Retail Brands
item.description:
Canadian Tire Corp., a major Canadian retail conglomerate, has reported a data breach affecting its e-commerce customers. Discovered on October 2, 2025, the incident involved unauthorized access to a single database serving multiple brands, including Canadian Tire, SportChek, Mark's/L'Équipeur, and ...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Data Breach, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: US Cyber Threat Sharing Law 'CISA 2015' Expires, Creating Potential Intelligence Gap
item.description:
The Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015 (CISA 2015), a foundational U.S. law that provided liability protections to encourage private companies to share cyber threat data with the government, expired on October 1, 2025. Amidst a government shutdown and a block by Senator Rand Paul, lawmake...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: UK's NCSC Warns of 'Alarming' Rise in Cyberattacks, Doubling in Past Year
item.description:
The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) revealed in its 2025 annual review that it managed 204 "nationally significant" cyberattacks over the past year, more than double the 89 incidents from the previous year. The agency attributed the alarming surge to increasing threats from state-sponsore...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Methodist Homes Discloses Healthcare Data Breach Affecting Nearly 26,000
item.description:
Methodist Homes of Alabama & Northwest Florida, a senior living and healthcare provider, announced on October 8, 2025, that it suffered a data breach affecting 25,579 individuals. The incident, which occurred over a 12-day period in October 2024, resulted in unauthorized access to sensitive personal...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: Signal Threatens to Exit EU Market if "Chat Control" Mass Surveillance Bill Passes
item.description:
Meredith Whittaker, the president of the Signal Foundation, has declared that the encrypted messaging service will withdraw from the European Union if the controversial 'Chat Control' legislation is enacted. The proposed law, which faces a critical vote on October 14, would mandate that communicatio...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Warns of Widespread Flaws in Industrial Control Systems from Major Vendors
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has released a series of advisories warning of numerous vulnerabilities in Industrial Control Systems (ICS) from prominent vendors, including Rockwell Automation, Hitachi Energy, Mitsubishi Electric, and Delta Electronics. These flaws ...

Severity: High

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Vulnerability, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: G7 Cyber Experts Issue Statement on Managing AI Risks in Financial Sector
item.description:
The G7 Cyber Expert Group (CEG) has issued a formal statement on the cybersecurity implications of Artificial Intelligence (AI) within the financial sector. Released on October 6, 2025, the document highlights the dual nature of AI, acknowledging its potential to bolster cyber defenses while also wa...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →
